description: Phase 3: Full game state persistence (save/load/auto-save) routed through tRPC gameRouter (no browser-direct DB), versioned save format with Zod validation and migrations, and a comprehensive debug menu gated to NODE_ENV=development. Depends on PRD-01 and PRD-02.

testingDirectives.framework: Vitest (already configured at vitest.config.ts, run via `pnpm test`)
requirements: Every new module MUST have a co-located test file (e.g., GameState.ts → src/game/__tests__/engine/GameState.test.ts). Story is not complete without tests.
Unit tests are the minimum quality bar. `pnpm test` must pass before committing.
The full verification command is: `pnpm test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.

testPatterns.stateModules: Test all public methods: get/set/serialize/restore. Verify onChange callbacks fire. Verify invalid inputs are rejected. Use the existing GameState.test.ts as a pattern.
testPatterns.persistence: Mock the tRPC adapter (vi.fn()). Test save/load/migrate flows without network. Verify dirty tracking, auto-save timing, and error handling.
testPatterns.migrations: Test each migration transform with fixture data. Verify unknown versions throw. Test full migration chain (0.1 → latest).
testPatterns.debugMenu: Test that debug value changes propagate through GameState/runtimeConfig. Test that debug state is excluded from serialization.

testingDirectives.debugBridge: window.__catHerderDebug is exposed in dev mode (Game.ts). It provides: world, gameState, eventBus, physicsEngine, getPlayerEntity(), getActiveCats(), tick(n) for deterministic physics stepping. E2E scripts can use this via dev-browser.
antiPatterns: Do NOT mark a story as passing without `pnpm test` succeeding.
Do NOT write tests that import Three.js or require DOM — use mocks for SceneManager/InputManager.
Do NOT test implementation details (private methods) — test behavior through public APIs.
Do NOT skip tests to make CI green — fix the underlying issue.

userStories.title: GameState full implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a central state store that tracks all game data with change detection so UI updates reactively and serialization captures everything.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/state/GameState.ts exposes get<T>(path: string): T, set<T>(path: string, value: T), onChange<T>(path: string, callback: (newVal: T, oldVal: T) => void): Unsubscribe, serialize(): SaveData, restore(data: SaveData).

State shape includes: player (appearance, position, stats.level, stats.health, stats.maxHealth, yarn, oxygen, abilities, inventory); world (currentMapId, activeCats[], hiddenTerrain[], resourceNodes[]); session (totalPlaytimeMs, isSwimming, isDiving).
set() triggers registered onChange() callbacks for that path.
serialize() excludes transient state (session data, debug overrides) and returns a JSON-safe object.
restore() hydrates all state from a saved snapshot and triggers change callbacks.
State validation: set() rejects invalid values (e.g., negative health, yarn below 0).
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Save data format & versioning
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a versioned save format so future schema changes don't break existing saves.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/state/SaveData.ts defines a SaveData interface and a Zod schema for runtime validation: { version: '0.1', character, world, session }.
src/game/state/migrations.ts implements migrateIfNeeded(data: unknown): SaveData.
Migration pattern: '0.1' -> '0.2' -> ... each as a transform function.
If version is current: no-op. Older: chain migrations. Unknown: throw with a user-presentable error.
Stub migration '0.1' -> '0.2' included as a template.
The same SaveData Zod schema is also used to validate the saveData input on gameRouter.upsertSave — schema lives in a shared module so client and server agree.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Persistence module (save/load via tRPC)
userStories.description: As a player, I want my game to auto-save so I never lose progress.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/state/Persistence.ts exposes save(): Promise<void>, load(): Promise<SaveData | null>, startAutoSave(intervalMs: number), stopAutoSave(), forceSave(): Promise<void>.
Persistence depends on the GameTrpcAdapter introduced in PRD-01 — calls trpc.upsertSave({ version, saveData }) and trpc.getSave(). No direct fetch() to Neon, no PostgREST.
save(): calls gameState.serialize(), runs the SaveData Zod schema as a client-side guard, sends through the adapter.
load(): calls trpc.getSave(), runs migrateIfNeeded(), returns SaveData | null.
startAutoSave(30000): timer that calls save() only if state is dirty (changed since last save).
Dirty tracking: isDirty flag set by GameState.set(), cleared after successful save.
Position updates only mark dirty every 5 seconds (prevents excessive saves from movement jitter).
forceSave(): immediate save regardless of dirty flag — used by the manual save button.

window.beforeunload handler calls a fire-and-forget save: prefer navigator.sendBeacon() against a dedicated Next.js route handler POST /api/game/beacon-save that re-uses the same upsert logic with WorkOS session validation. Fall back gracefully if sendBeacon is unavailable.
isSaving state and lastSavedAt timestamp exposed for HUD.
Save errors surface as SAVE_FAILED events on EventBus (HUD shows the indicator from US-209) — they never crash the game loop.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Load & restore flow
userStories.description: As a returning player, I want to resume where I left off when I open the game.
acceptanceCriteria: On /play mount (after the auth + character check from PRD-01): Persistence.load() is called.
If no save exists AND no character: redirect to Character Creator (PRD-01 US-015).
If character exists but no save: start new game at map spawn point with default state (10 yarn, full health).
If save exists: gameState.restore(saveData) -> player entity spawned at saved position with saved stats, inventory, yarn.
Active cats from save: re-summoned at saved positions (validated against terrain — invalid -> silently dismissed at restore time).
Resource node cooldowns from save: restored (mid-cooldown nodes remain mid-cooldown with remaining time).
Loading state shown while fetching (loading screen or spinner from GameCanvas).
If load fails (network / TRPCError): show retry option, allow starting new game as fallback. Never silently fall back to a default game — users must consent to losing a save.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Debug Menu — Player tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to modify player stats in real-time so I can test different progression states.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/ui/DebugMenu.ts opens on Ctrl+D only when process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' — the menu code itself is gated behind the env check at construction time so it tree-shakes out of production bundles.
Semi-transparent dark panel overlaying the game (does NOT pause the game).

Player tab contains: Level selector (0-10), Health setter (0 to maxHealth), Yarn setter (0-99), Teleport (X/Z + button), Speed multiplier slider (0.5x-3.0x) modifying runtimeConfig.movement.walkSpeed, God mode toggle.
All changes apply immediately via GameState.set() or runtimeConfig mutation.
Changes emit DEBUG_VALUE_CHANGED events on EventBus.
Debug values are session-only — never written to the save file.
Ctrl+D toggles menu closed.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Debug Menu — Cats tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to test cat abilities without resource constraints so I can iterate on cat behavior rapidly.
acceptanceCriteria: Cats tab contains: Force-summon buttons (one per cat type, ignores yarn cost and cat limit), Yarn override input, Cat limit override (1-10), Dismiss all button, Cat behavior state display (Idle/Active/Expired) for each active cat.
Force-summoned cats appear at player's current position + 2u offset.
All changes apply immediately.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Debug Menu — World tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to manipulate world state so I can test edge cases and balance.
acceptanceCriteria: World tab contains: Time scale slider (0.25x-4.0x) affecting dt for all systems EXCEPT InputManager, Reload map button -> MapManager.loadMap(TestMap), Show collision wireframes toggle, Show entity count + breakdown by component type, Resource controls (Fill all nodes / Empty all nodes), Spawn resource node (type dropdown + position + Spawn button).
Collision wireframes render as a separate Three.js layer (toggled via SceneManager).
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Debug Menu — Session tab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to control save/load and view session diagnostics.
acceptanceCriteria: Session tab contains: Force Save -> Persistence.forceSave() with success/failure feedback, Force Load -> Persistence.load() + gameState.restore(), Reset All State -> calls gameRouter.deleteSave (PRD-01 US-014) and reloads with default state (keeps character), Last saved timestamp display (reactive), FPS counter, Entity count, Session time, Auto-save status with toggle.
Reset All State requires confirmation dialog ('Are you sure? This deletes your save.').
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: HUD save indicator
userStories.description: As a player, I want subtle save feedback so I know my progress is being saved without being interrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: Small save icon (disk/cloud) appears briefly in corner when auto-save completes successfully.
Icon shows for 2s then fades.
On save failure: icon shows with red X, persists for 5s, surfaces the underlying error in a tooltip.
Indicator does not block gameplay or cover HUD elements.
pnpm typecheck passes.
